steps.title: Use the context
steps.content: The motorman is chafing in his box, thinking of the drudging lot of the laboring man. He registered discontent — meaning he showed his discontent (it was visible on him).

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
"Registered" here needs to mean showed outwardly — the motorman's discontent was visible. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — enrolled: "He enrolled discontent" — enrolled means signed up or joined a list. Discontent is a feeling, not something you register for. ✗
  • Choice B — valued: "He valued discontent" — valued means prized or appreciated. But the motorman is suffering, not cherishing his discontent. ✗
  • Choice C — disregarded: "He disregarded discontent" — disregarded means ignored. This is the opposite: his discontent was shown, not ignored. ✗
  • Choice D — displayed: "He displayed discontent" — displayed means showed outwardly. The motorman was chafing and brooding, making his discontent visible. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
